given: orchestrator passes --spec docs/dev/specs/spec-foo.md to /dev and BA returns a context.json variant
when: orchestrator runs Step 5 patch in commands/dev.md against each fixture
then: post-patch context.json.spec_path equals 'docs/dev/specs/spec-foo.md' in all four positive cases (absent / null / empty / different); in the negative case where orchestrator-resolved spec_path == null, the patch does NOT invent a value

fixtures.note: Codex iter-2 C3: condition is orchestrator-resolved spec_path == null (covers both --spec-not-passed and --spec-passed-but-auto-detect-returned-null subcases). Patch must NOT invent a value.

given: score-inject.sh emits INJECTION_PROOF block to QA subagent dispatch
when: QA produces its qa-report JSON for this cycle
then: qa-report contains all 4 fields: rank_acknowledged (non-empty string), range_acknowledged (non-empty string), recent_events_digest_acknowledged (8-char hex), score_injection_action (non-empty string >= 20 chars, not a forbidden placeholder)

given: command using ANSI-C quoting to splice a find write-action flag is submitted to pretool-bash-safety.sh, with a bulk-commit protected path argument
when: Layer 1.F evaluates the command using the canonical shlex.shlex(posix=True, punctuation_chars=True) tokenizer
then: hook DENIES (rc=2) every write-action family member after ANSI-C reassembly; not just -fprint

fixtures.note: Iter-1 baseline. Token after ANSI-C reassembly via shlex.shlex is $-fprint; write-action veto must match this form per iter-1 codex F2.

Iter-2 codex C9. Prevents a special-case $-fprint-only patch from passing — write-action handler must generalize across the find write-action family ({-delete, -exec, -execdir, -fprint, -fprint0, -fprintf, -fls, -ok, -okdir, ...}).

given: commands invoking read-only grep against the protected flag with various shell metacharacters in patterns, plus active subshell/process-sub forms
when: Layer 1.F evaluates each command using the canonical tokenizer
then: ALLOW (rc=0) when metacharacter is enclosed in single quotes inside an argument to a pure-read verb; DENY (rc=2) when the metacharacter is an active unquoted or double-quoted subshell/process-sub form

allow_fixtures.note: Iter-2 OBJ-3 extension. shlex token: a;b
Iter-2 OBJ-3 extension. shlex token: a && b
Iter-2 OBJ-3 extension (single quotes inhibit $() expansion). shlex token: $(cmd). MUST be distinguished from unquoted active form in cases (g)(h) below.
Iter-2 codex C8 ALLOW counterpart. shlex token: <(cmd)

deny_fixtures.note: Iter-2 codex C6. Unquoted $() is an active subshell; the leading $ survives shlex stripping and is detectable via per-token prefix check or raw-text inspection.

Iter-2 codex C6. CRITICAL: double-quotes do NOT inhibit $() expansion in bash; without raw-text quote-state tracking, shlex with posix=True will strip the double quotes and yield the token as if it were single-quoted. Implementation MUST distinguish this from single-quoted case (d) — either via raw-text pass before tokenization, or by inspecting the original command-text for unquoted/double-quoted $( substring.

check.should_have_caveat: Backtick `cmd` and here-string <<< distinguishing (quoted vs unquoted) is NOT in the must-have set per iter-2 codex C7 — shlex tokenizes them identically. Dev MAY add raw-text inspection for these if cost is low (SHOULD_HAVE).

given: bare `cat` against a protected-path glob using each of 4 POSIX shell-glob bracket forms
when: the Layer 1.F entry-gate regex at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:662 evaluates each command
then: the entry gate matches in all 4 cases, allowing the command to enter Layer 1.F evaluation; since cat is in the pure-read allowlist with no compound shape, all exit rc=0

fixtures.note: Glob char *. Entry gate MUST match; pure-read allows.
Bracket expression POSIX negation form. The ! is part of bracket syntax, not a separate glob char. Implementation regex (if used) must match literal !.

check.note_for_dev: Iter-1 codex F5 rejected the regex [A-Za-z0-9_-*?[\]]* as it produces grep: Invalid range end. Two valid impls: (1) `grep -F` substring match on both protected-path prefixes, OR (2) validated regex with properly escaped glob chars. Iter-2 codex C10: bracket chars are glob syntax not regex char classes — regex impl must escape [, ], !, *, ?.

given: each of the 4 glob forms in AC-05 with a compound `;rm` appendage
when: the entry-gate regex AND compound detector evaluate the command
then: hook DENIES (rc=2) in all 4 cases — entry gate matches the glob path AND compound `;` is detected

check.discriminating_note: Iter-1 codex F4 + iter-2 OBJ-5. Before fix: all 4 cases exit rc=0 because entry gate misses the glob char and Layer 1.F is entirely skipped. After fix: all 4 cases exit rc=2 because entry gate matches AND compound is detected.

when: dev appends Cycle 4/5/6 sections from completion-20260524-172805.md, completion-20260524-205206.md, completion-20260525-095242.md
then: spec Sections 2-8 contain ### Cycle 4, ### Cycle 5, ### Cycle 6 headers with NON-EMPTY content beneath each header sourced from the three completion reports. Each cycle header must have at least one line of implementation summary.

given: compound commands with no whitespace around the shell separator
when: Layer 1.F evaluates the command using the canonical shlex.shlex(posix=True, punctuation_chars=True) tokenizer
then: hook DENIES (rc=2) all 4 cases — punctuation_chars=True correctly splits unspaced separators that plain shlex.split would glue to a token

fixtures.note: Iter-2 OBJ-2. Plain shlex.split yields ['ls', '<FLAG>;python3', ...] — ; glued to token. punctuation_chars=True yields ['ls', '<FLAG>', ';', 'python3', ...].
Iter-2 codex C5. || is in the existing Layer 1.F compound separator contract; must be in regression matrix.

given: malformed command with unterminated quoting submitted to pretool-bash-safety.sh
when: Layer 1.F invokes the canonical tokenizer and exhausts list(lex)
then: list(lex) raises ValueError (typically 'No closing quotation'); the try/except wrapper falls through to DENY (rc=2) — never silent allow

fixtures.note: Iter-2 OBJ-4. shlex.shlex(posix=True, punctuation_chars=True) raises ValueError: No closing quotation when list(lex) consumes the lexer.

check.implementation_hint_for_dev:
Iter-2 codex C11: the helper MUST exhaust the lexer via `list(lex)` (or equivalent for-loop consuming all tokens) inside a `try/except ValueError: deny()` block. Constructing `shlex.shlex(...)` alone does NOT raise. Sample skeleton:

```python
import shlex
try:
    lex = shlex.shlex(cmd, posix=True, punctuation_chars=True)
    lex.commenters = ''
    lex.whitespace_split = True
    tokens = list(lex)
except ValueError:
    deny('bulk-commit-auth-flag-write: tokenizer ValueError (fail-closed)')
    sys.exit(2)
```
